RawMarkdown:
## INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AGREEMENT
THIS INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AGREEMENT (“Agreement”) is dated as of January 20th, 2026 (“Effective Date”), by and between MORTGAGE AUTOMATOR SOFTWARE INC. (“Mortgage Automator”), and Scott Galloway (“Contractor”).
The parties agree as follows:
1. Work to be Performed.
Contractor agrees to perform certain quality assurance testing and project work for Mortgage Automator or Mortgage Automator’s affiliated entities (“Projects”), and shall be required to report in writing to Mortgage Automator, with whatever frequency Mortgage Automator may require, to keep Mortgage Automator informed about Contractor’s activities under this Agreement. A description of the initial Project is found in a Statement of Work (“SOW”) submitted by Contractor to Mortgage Automator, incorporated herein by this reference, and attached hereto as Exhibit A. From time to time, Contractor and Mortgage Automator may agree to enter into additional SOWs.
2. Period for Performance.
This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and continue on for twelve (12) months (“Term”). This Agreement is subject to the termination provisions in Section 8.
3. Compensation.
3.1 Rate. Contractor shall be compensated at an hourly rate of $150.00 USD for performing the work on each Project in a satisfactory manner as determined by the Staff Contact. Time will be pre-allocated in 80-hour block retainers of time. Contractor shall not be compensated for more than $200,000.00 during the Term.
3.2 Contractor Invoices. Contractor will submit detailed invoices for payment within three (3) business days of the last day of each month during the Term. Mortgage Automator shall satisfy all undisputed Contractor invoices no later than thirty (30) days following receipt.
3.3 Staff Contact. Mortgage Automator’s initial representative (“Staff Contact”) is Jason Alexander. The Staff Contact may be changed by Mortgage Automator at any time and Mortgage Automator will notify Contractor of any such change.
4. Scope of Work.
The SOW will consist of a series of projects which will be assigned to Contractor by the Staff Contact. If it is practicable to do so, Contractor will describe the work required to complete the Project, provide Mortgage Automator with an assessment of how many days it will take to complete the Project and commit to a deadline for completion. If at any time during the performance of a Project the Contractor needs to make a change to the completion date or number of days required, Contractor will notify Mortgage Automator in writing (email to Staff Contact is acceptable). Contractor shall promptly inform Mortgage Automator in writing of any actual or potential delay in the timely performance of the work and the reason for such delay.
5. Non-Solicitation of Employees and Clients.
Contractor shall be prohibited from influencing any Mortgage Automator employee to terminate his or her employment with Mortgage Automator and from influencing any Mortgage Automator customer to curtail its business relationship with Mortgage Automator.
6. Intellectual Property / Work Made for Hire.
Contractor acknowledges and agrees that any intellectual property rights which may be derived from the Projects or work process shall be the sole property of Mortgage Automator and that Contractor shall cooperate with Mortgage Automator in its protection of such rights.
7. Independent Contractor Status.
The relationship of Contractor to Mortgage Automator is that of an independent contractor, and nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as creating any other relationship. Contractor is not eligible for any employee benefits and is responsible for all applicable taxes. No worker’s compensation insurance shall be obtained by Mortgage Automator covering Contractor.
8. Termination.
Either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice. Mortgage Automator shall compensate Contractor for all work agreed upon and delivered satisfactorily prior to termination.
### 9. Conflict of Interest.
Contractor represents and warrants that no conflict exists. Any actual or potential conflict must be disclosed immediately.
10. Indemnification.
Contractor shall indemnify and hold harmless Mortgage Automator from any and all costs, expenses, claims, and losses arising out of Contractor’s performance, breach, actions, or infringement claims.
11. Confidentiality.
All Mortgage Automator software, systems, and confidential information are proprietary and shall not be disclosed, copied, reverse engineered, or misused.
12. Choice of Law.
This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein.
13. Survival.
Confidentiality and indemnity obligations survive termination.
14. Notice.
### MORTGAGE AUTOMATOR SOFTWARE INC.
160 Bloor St E., Suite 1010
Toronto, ON M4W 1B9
Canada
15. Entire Agreement.
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and may only be modified in writing signed by both parties.
16. Severability.
If any provision is found unenforceable, it shall be severed without affecting the remaining provisions.
### MORTGAGE AUTOMATOR SOFTWARE INC.
By: _________________________
Jason Alexander
Chief Executive Officer
### SCOTT GALLOWAY
By: _________________________
Scott Galloway (01/20/2023)
## Exhibit A – Statement of Work
The work to be performed by the Contractor will be as an Lead .NET Developer, specifically helping with engineering with AI, AI implementations, AI automations, and AI processes.
